sources: EPIC for America: Final FY2026 Appropriations Levels
Senate Appropriations Committee: FY2026 bill summaries
meta.notes: UBI figures assume 50% of savings distributed equally to all 335M Americans. Mandatory program figures are CBO projections; discretionary figures are final enacted appropriations. Some sub-agency figures are estimates derived from appropriations bill summaries.

items.name: Social Security — Retirement (OASI)
items.description: Monthly retirement checks for 57M+ retirees and their dependents. The biggest single line item in the federal budget.
items.cut_note: Directly benefits 57M+ recipients who paid into the system their entire working lives.

items.name: Social Security — Disability (SSDI)
items.description: Disability income for 8M+ Americans unable to work due to severe physical or mental conditions.
items.cut_note: Supports Americans with verified severe disabilities — cutting would eliminate income for 8M people.

items.name: Medicare
items.description: Federal health insurance for all Americans 65+ and the disabled. Covers hospital stays, outpatient care, prescription drugs.
items.cut_note: Every American who paid taxes expects this in retirement. Cutting it would leave 65M+ without insurance.

items.name: Medicaid
items.description: Health coverage for 90M low-income Americans, children, pregnant women, elderly, and disabled. Co-funded with states.
items.cut_note: Eliminating federal Medicaid would leave 90M people without healthcare coverage.

items.name: Net Interest on the National Debt
items.description: Interest payments owed on $36T+ of accumulated national debt. Not a program — it's a bill that comes due no matter what.
items.cut_note: Defaulting on debt payments would crash global financial markets and make borrowing nearly impossible.

items.name: Department of Defense
items.description: Total national defense spending including Army, Navy, Air Force, Space Force, Marines, and all defense agencies. Includes $152B in reconciliation funding.

items.name: U.S. Army
items.description: Ground combat forces, aviation, logistics, and army reserve components.

items.name: U.S. Navy & Marine Corps
items.description: Naval fleet operations, shipbuilding, carrier strike groups, and Marine Corps ground forces.

items.name: U.S. Air Force & Space Force
items.description: Air superiority, strategic bombers, intercontinental missiles, satellite operations, and space systems.

items.name: F-35 Joint Strike Fighter Program
items.description: Annual procurement and sustainment costs for the F-35 — the most expensive weapons program in US history at ~$1.7T lifetime cost.

items.name: Nuclear Weapons (NNSA)
items.description: Design, production, and maintenance of the entire US nuclear arsenal. Managed by the National Nuclear Security Administration.

items.name: Department of Veterans Affairs
items.description: Healthcare, disability compensation, education benefits, and housing assistance for 9M+ veterans.
items.cut_note: Cutting veterans benefits after asking people to risk their lives for the country is politically and morally indefensible.

items.name: Veterans Benefits — Mandatory
items.description: Disability compensation, pension, education (GI Bill), home loan guarantees, and life insurance for veterans — mandatory entitlement spending.
items.cut_note: Earned benefits for military service. Politically untouchable.

items.name: Dept. of Health and Human Services
items.agency: Department of Health and Human Services
items.description: Discretionary HHS funding covering NIH research, CDC, FDA, mental health, substance abuse, child welfare, and public health infrastructure.

items.name: National Institutes of Health (NIH)
items.agency: Department of Health and Human Services
items.description: The world's largest funder of biomedical research. Funds cancer, Alzheimer's, diabetes, infectious disease, and basic science research.

items.name: Centers for Disease Control (CDC)
items.agency: Department of Health and Human Services
items.description: Tracks and responds to disease outbreaks, runs vaccination programs, monitors chronic disease, and maintains national health statistics.

items.name: Food and Drug Administration (FDA)
items.agency: Department of Health and Human Services
items.description: Approves and regulates drugs, medical devices, food safety, and vaccines before they reach consumers.

items.name: Department of Transportation
items.description: Highway and bridge construction, aviation safety (FAA), rail, transit grants, pipeline safety, and maritime operations.

items.name: Department of Education
items.description: Title I grants to low-income schools, Pell Grants for college, special education (IDEA), student loan administration, and civil rights enforcement.

items.name: Department of Homeland Security
items.description: Border protection, immigration enforcement, FEMA disaster relief, Secret Service, TSA airport security, and Coast Guard.

items.name: Customs & Border Protection (CBP)
items.description: Border patrol agents, ports of entry inspection, and customs enforcement. The largest law enforcement agency in the US.

items.name: FEMA — Federal Emergency Management Agency
items.description: Coordinates federal disaster response and recovery for hurricanes, wildfires, floods, earthquakes, and other emergencies.

items.name: Transportation Security Administration (TSA)
items.description: Airport security screening for 2M+ passengers per day at 440+ commercial airports.

items.name: State Dept. & USAID — Foreign Affairs
items.description: US embassies worldwide, diplomatic operations, foreign aid programs, international development, and humanitarian assistance.

items.name: Foreign Aid (USAID programs)
items.description: Direct foreign aid to other countries: development assistance, global health, food security, democracy promotion, and humanitarian relief.

items.name: Department of the Interior
items.description: Manages 500M+ acres of federal land, national parks, wildlife refuges, tribal affairs, and natural resource extraction on public land.

items.name: Department of Justice (DOJ)
items.description: Federal law enforcement including the FBI, DEA, ATF, US Marshals, Bureau of Prisons, and federal prosecutors.

items.name: Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI)
items.description: Counterterrorism, counterintelligence, cybercrime, organized crime, public corruption, and major violent crime investigations.

items.name: Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA)
items.description: Enforces US drug laws and targets domestic and international drug trafficking organizations.

items.name: NASA
items.description: Space exploration, Artemis moon program, Mars missions, earth observation satellites, aeronautics research, and ISS operations.

items.name: Department of Energy (DOE)
items.description: Nuclear weapons research (NNSA), national laboratories, energy technology R&D, grid security, and cleanup of Cold War nuclear sites.

items.name: Department of Agriculture (USDA)
items.description: Farm subsidies (discretionary portion), food safety inspection, rural development, forest service, and agricultural research.

items.name: Dept. of Housing & Urban Development (HUD)
items.agency: Department of Housing and Urban Development
items.description: Section 8 housing vouchers, public housing, community development block grants, and fair housing enforcement.

items.name: Department of the Treasury / IRS
items.description: IRS tax collection and enforcement, financial crimes enforcement, US Mint, currency printing, and financial regulation.

items.name: Department of Commerce
items.description: Census Bureau, NOAA weather forecasting, NIST standards, Patent Office, economic development, and international trade enforcement.

items.name: NOAA — National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration
items.description: Weather forecasting (National Weather Service), hurricane and tornado warnings, climate monitoring, fisheries management, and satellite operations.

items.name: Department of Labor
items.description: Workplace safety (OSHA), job training programs, wage enforcement, Bureau of Labor Statistics, and unemployment system oversight.

items.name: National Science Foundation (NSF)
items.description: Funds basic scientific research across all disciplines at US universities — the original source of funding for the internet, GPS, and touchscreens.

items.name: Environmental Protection Agency (EPA)
items.description: Regulates air and water quality, toxic waste cleanup (Superfund), pesticide safety, and environmental enforcement.

items.name: Financial Services & General Govt
items.description: Federal courts, IRS enforcement, Executive Office of the President, GSA, Small Business Administration, and other government operations.

items.name: Legislative Branch (Congress)
items.description: Operating budgets for the House, Senate, Capitol Police, Library of Congress, Congressional Budget Office, and Government Accountability Office.

items.name: Small Business Administration (SBA)
items.description: Loan guarantees and technical assistance for small businesses, disaster loans, and government contracting programs.

items.name: CPB — Public Broadcasting (NPR / PBS)
items.description: Federal funding for NPR, PBS, and ~1,500 local public radio and TV stations. Politically controversial despite being a rounding error.

items.name: SNAP — Food Stamps
items.description: Monthly food assistance for 42M low-income Americans averaging $6/day per person. Largest US food assistance program.

items.name: Supplemental Security Income (SSI)
items.description: Cash assistance for elderly and disabled Americans with little to no income. Average benefit ~$700/month.
items.cut_note: Supports the poorest elderly and disabled Americans with no other income source.

items.name: Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)
items.description: Refundable tax credit for low-to-moderate income workers. The largest anti-poverty program for working families. Average credit ~$2,500.

items.name: Child Tax Credit (refundable portion)
items.description: Refundable tax credit for families with children. Up to $2,000 per child under 17.

items.name: Unemployment Insurance
items.description: Weekly income replacement for recently unemployed workers. Funded jointly by federal and state payroll taxes.

items.name: TANF — Temporary Assistance for Needy Families
items.agency: Department of Health and Human Services
items.description: Cash welfare for very low-income families with children. Comes with work requirements and a 5-year lifetime limit.

items.name: ACA Marketplace Subsidies
items.description: Premium tax credits and cost-sharing subsidies for ~15M Americans buying health insurance on the Obamacare marketplace.

items.name: CHIP — Children's Health Insurance Program
items.description: Health coverage for 7M+ children in families that earn too much for Medicaid but can't afford private insurance.
items.cut_note: Provides healthcare to 7M children in working families. Cutting this is politically radioactive.

items.name: Federal & Military Retirement Pensions
items.description: Defined benefit pensions for retired federal civilian employees and military personnel who served before pension reforms.
items.cut_note: Contractual obligations to people who already served. Cutting would require breaking existing contracts.

items.name: Federal Housing Assistance (mandatory)
items.agency: Department of Housing and Urban Development
items.description: Mandatory housing assistance including Section 8 voucher commitments, rental assistance contracts, and public housing operating subsidies.
